text.title: RAG Feature Acceptance Contract — Decision Tree
text.summary: Routes by intent-count, SME availability, and eval-set size — drives whether to author the contract, defer, or downgrade to preference-label flow.

decision-tree.root-question: How many distinguishable intent classes does query analysis surface?

conclusion.verdict: downgrade — single-intent feature does not need this contract; use recall@k gate instead

branch.question: Is an authoritative SME committed for ≥4h of rubric work?

conclusion.verdict: STOP — without SME the contract is vibe-check; escalate or downgrade to preference-label + Elo

branch.question: Is the labelled eval set ≥50 queries per intent?

conclusion.verdict: defer — thresholds cannot be defended on <50 samples; sample more first

branch.question: Is matched online telemetry live (thumbs-down OR escalation OR CSAT)?

conclusion.verdict: instrument telemetry first, then author contract; offline-only is forbidden
author contract; collect three signatures; wire recontract triggers; register as CI ship gate

example.trace: 2 intents + SME + 80-query eval + thumbs-down live → full contract
example.code: intent_count=4 → q-sme → sme_available=true → q-eval → eval_set_size=80 → q-telemetry → online_signal=true → r5-recontract-trigger verdict applied
intent_count=2 → q-sme → sme_available=false → r2-three-signatures verdict: escalate
